text.title: Agentic Portfolio Workflow
text.summary: Three-pass structured pipeline for computing cross-product horizon allocation and generating a signed reconciliation memo.

section.title: Three-Pass Pipeline

rule.statement: Pass 1 (Product-PM pass): for each product, a sonnet agent reads the product backlog, OKRs, and recent retros and emits {product, h1_pct, h2_pct, h3_pct, key_evidence}. Weights are not yet cross-product.
rule.rationale: Starting per-product isolates each PM's plan before aggregation. Cross-product trade-offs introduced too early cause defensiveness that blocks honest disclosure.

rule.statement: Pass 2 (Portfolio-PM pass): an opus agent aggregates across products, weighs engineering cost per product, and detects role-level anti-patterns. Output: JSON with per-product recommended horizon mix, portfolio totals, PM role findings, and a reallocation memo of at most 6 bullets naming cuts and bets.
rule.rationale: Opus for the aggregation pass because cross-cutting reasoning across multiple products with dependencies requires sustained multi-step reasoning that cheaper models abbreviate.

rule.statement: Pass 3 (Reconciliation): output a structured memo that names which product gets cut, which gets accelerated, and which PM owns the H3 budget for the next quarter. The memo goes to a human portfolio-PM for sign-off; never auto-applied.
rule.rationale: Agent-only portfolio decisions are an anti-pattern. The matrix is decision support; the portfolio-PM holds the final allocation authority.

rule.statement: All three passes must produce JSON output; the reconciliation memo is the only prose artifact, and it must have a top-level cuts: section before the prose body.
rule.rationale: Reconciliation memos bury the cut in prose. A required cuts: field at the top forces the agent to make trade-offs legible before the narrative justification.

section.reference: LLM prompt for portfolio-PM pass: aggregates per-product allocations, detects bimodal and H1-only patterns.
LLM prompt for single-product PM defending allocation within portfolio targets.

section.title: Pre-Run Preparation

rule.statement: Pre-compute the cross-product dependency graph as cross_product_deps.csv (columns: item_id, depends_on_product, blocking_severity) and pass it to the portfolio-PM agent. Never let the agent infer dependencies from product descriptions alone.
rule.rationale: LLMs will not detect cross-product dependencies unless supplied as edges in the input. Without a dependency graph, the agent invents synergies that do not exist and misses real blockers.

rule.statement: Pass owner_count (number of humans running the portfolio) alongside product data. If the stated allocation is infeasible for the headcount, the agent must flag "headcount insufficient" rather than pretend the ratio is achievable.
rule.rationale: Solo-founder portfolios mislead the model — it will recommend a clean 70/20/10 split for one human running 6 products as if that is executable.

section.reference: Bash script that detects single-product vs portfolio PM anti-patterns from a CSV of pm,product,horizon,eng_cost_usd rows before quarterly review.
